findings.title: nanoGPT: Minimalist GPT Training Framework
findings.one_liner: A simple, fast, and hackable repository for training and finetuning GPT-style large language models from scratch.
findings.mental_model: nanoGPT implements the core GPT-2 transformer architecture in `model.py`, emphasizing clarity and performance. The `train.py` script orchestrates the entire training pipeline, including data loading from pre-processed binary files, model instantiation, optimization, and distributed training. Users define model and training parameters via configuration files or command-line arguments, and trained models can then be used for text generation via `sample.py`.

concepts.name: GPT Model Architecture
concepts.description: The neural network definition of the GPT model, including its core components like Layer Normalization, Causal Self-Attention, and MLP blocks, arranged into a stack of Transformer Blocks. It also includes the `GPTConfig` dataclass for parameterizing the model.

concepts.name: Training Loop & Orchestration
concepts.description: The main script responsible for managing the end-to-end training process. This includes loading data, initializing or resuming model training, setting up the optimizer and learning rate schedule, performing forward and backward passes, logging, evaluation, and handling distributed data parallel (DDP) training.

concepts.name: Data Preparation
concepts.description: The process of converting raw text datasets (e.g., Shakespeare, OpenWebText) into a tokenized, numerical, and efficiently loadable binary format (`.bin` files) for training. This step typically involves tokenization (e.g., using `tiktoken` for BPE tokens) and serialization.

concepts.name: Configuration Management
concepts.description: The mechanism for defining and overriding hyperparameters and training settings. This involves default values defined in `train.py`, Python configuration files (`config/*.py`), and command-line arguments parsed by `configurator.py`.

concepts.name: Text Generation (Sampling)
concepts.description: The process of loading a trained GPT model checkpoint and using it to generate new text sequences given a prompt. This involves autoregressive sampling, where the model predicts the next token based on the preceding sequence.

curriculum.title: Understanding nanoGPT: Minimalist GPT Training Framework
curriculum.mental_model: nanoGPT implements a clear and efficient version of the GPT-2 transformer architecture, focusing on making large language model training accessible. Students will learn to process text data, build the core GPT model, orchestrate its training loop, and finally generate text from a trained model.

concepts.name: Data Preparation
concepts.description: The process of converting raw text into tokenized, numerical, and efficiently loadable binary data for model training.

concepts.name: GPT Model Architecture
concepts.description: The neural network structure of the GPT model, composed of Layer Normalization, Causal Self-Attention, and MLP blocks within stacked Transformer Blocks.

concepts.name: Training Loop & Orchestration
concepts.description: The main process that manages model initialization, data loading, forward and backward passes, optimization, and logging to train the GPT model.

concepts.name: Text Generation (Sampling)
concepts.description: The technique of using a trained GPT model to autoregressively predict and generate new text sequences given an initial prompt.

notebooks.title: Preparing Text Data for LLMs
notebooks.description: Students will learn how to tokenize raw text and convert it into a binary format suitable for efficient loading by an LLM, mimicking the `nanoGPT` data preparation pipeline.
learning_objectives: By the end, students will be able to tokenize raw text using BPE.
Students will understand how text is represented numerically for neural networks.
Students will be able to save tokenized data into an efficient binary format.
notebooks.exercise_description: Given a raw text file, students will implement a Python script to tokenize its content, encode it into integers, and save it as a binary file (e.g., `.bin`) using `numpy.memmap`.
notebooks.visualization_idea: Animate the process of converting a sentence into BPE tokens, then to integer IDs, and finally visualizing how these IDs are stored sequentially in a binary file.

notebooks.title: Deconstructing the GPT Model Architecture
notebooks.description: Students will build a simplified version of the core components of the GPT model, including attention, MLP, and the overall block structure, understanding how they fit together.
learning_objectives: By the end, students will be able to implement a Causal Self-Attention mechanism.
Students will understand the role of Layer Normalization and MLP in a Transformer block.
Students will be able to assemble these components into a full Transformer Block and a minimal GPT model.
notebooks.exercise_description: Students will implement the `CausalSelfAttention` and `Block` classes from scratch, given the `GPTConfig` parameters and PyTorch's `nn.Module` interface.
notebooks.visualization_idea: Draw an interactive diagram of a Transformer Block, highlighting the data flow from input embeddings through Layer Norm, Causal Self-Attention, another Layer Norm, and the MLP, showing how residuals are added.

notebooks.title: Orchestrating the LLM Training Loop
notebooks.description: Students will implement a basic training loop for a language model, covering data loading, forward and backward passes, optimizer steps, and basic logging.
learning_objectives: By the end, students will be able to load batches of data for training.
Students will understand the steps involved in one training iteration (forward, backward, optimize).
Students will be able to set up a basic optimizer and learning rate scheduler.
notebooks.exercise_description: Students will implement a `Trainer` class that orchestrates the training loop for a small `GPT` model, including batch loading, calculating loss, performing backpropagation, and updating model parameters.
notebooks.visualization_idea: Animate a single training step: data batch input -> model forward pass -> loss calculation -> backpropagation (gradient flow) -> optimizer step (parameter update).

notebooks.title: Generating Text with a Trained GPT
notebooks.description: Students will learn how to load a trained GPT model and use it to autoregressively generate new text sequences given a starting prompt.
learning_objectives: By the end, students will be able to load a pre-trained model checkpoint.
Students will understand the autoregressive sampling process for text generation.
Students will be able to generate text from a given prompt using a GPT model.
notebooks.exercise_description: Given a trained `MiniGPTModel` and a prompt string, students will implement the `generate` method that uses the model to predict the next token repeatedly until a specified length or end-of-sequence token is reached.
notebooks.visualization_idea: Illustrate the step-by-step token generation: prompt + model -> next token predicted -> next token appended to prompt -> repeat.

capstone.title: mini-nanoGPT: End-to-End LLM Pipeline
capstone.description: Students will build a simplified, end-to-end version of `nanoGPT`, capable of processing a small dataset, defining a minimalist GPT model, training it, and generating text. This project synthesizes all concepts learned throughout the course.

modules.name: MiniDataProcessor
modules.description: Handles tokenization of raw text and conversion to a binary format suitable for the mini-GPT model.
test_behaviors: Given a short text, `prepare_data` should create a `.bin` file.
The `.bin` file should contain numerical representations of tokens.

modules.name: MiniGPTModel
modules.description: A simplified GPT model, with fewer layers and parameters than a full GPT-2, but retaining the core transformer architecture.
test_behaviors: Calling `forward` with an input `idx` should return logits and potentially a loss.
Calling `generate` with a seed `idx` should produce a sequence of `max_new_tokens`.

modules.name: MiniTrainer
modules.description: Manages the training loop for the `MiniGPTModel`, including batching, optimization, and checkpointing.
test_behaviors: `train` should decrease the validation loss over a few iterations.
After training, a model checkpoint should be saved.

integration_test.description: Train a `MiniGPTModel` on a small, synthetic dataset (e.g., 'the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog') and then use the trained model to complete a prompt like 'the quick'.
integration_test.setup_code:
text_data = "the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. the lazy dog sleeps."
processor = MiniDataProcessor(vocab_size=65)
processor.prepare_data(text_data, "tiny_data.bin")
model = MiniGPTModel(vocab_size=65, block_size=8, n_layer=1, n_head=1, n_embd=16)
trainer = MiniTrainer(model, "tiny_data.bin", "tiny_data.bin", batch_size=4, learning_rate=1e-3, max_iters=100)
integration_test.success_metric: The generated text, when prompted with 'the quick', should contain words from the training data or plausible extensions (e.g., 'brown fox'). The generated sequence length should match the `max_new_tokens` parameter.
integration_test.expected_output_check:
model.load_state_dict(torch.load('latest_checkpoint.pt'))
start_idx = torch.tensor(processor.encode('the quick'), dtype=torch.long).unsqueeze(0)
generated_tokens = model.generate(start_idx, max_new_tokens=5)
generated_text = processor.decode(generated_tokens[0].tolist())
assert 'brown' in generated_text or 'fox' in generated_text or 'dog' in generated_text
